name: Text Align
content:
# Text Align
The [`text-align`(#d3e965)](/docs/weblab/text-align/) property specifies the horizontal alignment of text inside an element. This property is used to align inline content such as text within a block-level element.

examples.name: Centering Text in a Paragraph
examples.description: This centers all the text inside the paragraph.

examples.name: Justifying Text in a Block of Content
examples.description: This spreads the text out so both the left and right edges are aligned.

palette_params.name: value
palette_params.description:
Possible values include:

- **`left`** – Aligns text to the left. *(Default for left-to-right languages)*
- **`right`** – Aligns text to the right.
- **`center`** – Centers the text.
- **`justify`** – Stretches the lines so that each line has equal width (like in newspapers).

short_description:
Text Align
The text-align property specifies the horizontal alignment of text in an element. It is used to align text to the left, right, center, or justify it within its containing element.
tips:
- [`text-align`(#d3e965)](/docs/weblab/text-align/) is used to align **text inside a container**, like a paragraph (`

`) or heading (`

`). - `justify` spreads text evenly across a line, creating a clean, newspaper-style look. - The default alignment is usually `left` (in English and other left-to-right languages). - If you're writing in a right-to-left language (like Arabic or Hebrew), the default may be `right`.
